item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel vows to stay in south Lebanon, slams US-Iran peace deal as ministers reject agreement terms
item.description: Israeli government ministers strongly criticized the US-Iran agreement to end hostilities, declaring Israel not bound by the deal and vowing to maintain forces in southern Lebanon despite the ceasefire framework.

item.title: 🇬🇧 London court convicts two men in Russian-linked plot to firebomb properties tied to UK Prime Minister
item.description: Two men were convicted of an arson plot orchestrated by a mysterious Russian-speaking figure known as "El Money" who offered payment via Telegram to torch houses and a car linked to Keir Starmer.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran and US agree to halt war and reopen Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices tumbling
item.description: The preliminary agreement between Washington and Tehran to end military operations and reopen the critical oil shipping chokepoint sent crude prices sharply lower as global markets rallied on de-escalation.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Drone strikes killed over 1,000 civilians in Sudan in first five months of 2026, UN rights chief says
item.description: A senior UN official reported that drone strikes have killed more than 1,000 civilians in war-torn Sudan since January, highlighting the devastating toll of the ongoing civil conflict between the SAF and RSF.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese analysts flag Israel as wildcard in US-Iran deal, warn agreement may be messy and short-lived
item.description: As the US, Iran and Pakistan announced a peace agreement, Chinese analysts cautioned that Israel remains the biggest unpredictable factor and the deal could unravel if core issues are left unresolved.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Trump arrives at G7 summit in France after announcing US-Iran peace agreement
item.description: President Trump heads to the French Alps for the G7 summit with momentum from the US-Iran deal, but faces tensions with European allies over trade tariffs, Ukraine policy, and NATO commitments.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel refuses to withdraw from Lebanon as Iran demands frozen funds before final talks
item.description: Israel says its forces will remain in southern Lebanon despite the US-Iran peace deal, while Iran insists on receiving half of its frozen assets before proceeding to final negotiations.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK announces ban on social media for under-16s, following Australia model
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer confirmed plans to ban social media access for children under 16, targeting platforms like TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube, with rules expected to take effect in 2027.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian barrage on Ukraine kills 11 and damages landmark Kyiv religious site
item.description: Russia fired hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles at Ukrainian cities overnight, killing at least 11 including five rescuers in Kharkiv and damaging a renowned Orthodox monastery in Kyiv.

item.title: 📈 Global markets rally on hopes Iran peace deal will reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Stock markets surged and oil prices fell sharply after the US-Iran preliminary agreement raised expectations that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen, easing supply concerns and boosting investor confidence.

item.title: 🇺🇦⛪ Historic Kyiv Cathedral Set Ablaze as Russian Barrage Kills at Least 11 Across Ukraine
item.description: Russian missile strikes on multiple Ukrainian cities killed at least 11 people and set fire to the Dormition Cathedral within the 11th-century Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Zelensky accused Moscow of deliberately targeting the landmark monastery.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇸💰 Macron Rebukes Trump: "The US Does Not Decide European Law" Over Digital Tax Dispute
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron pushed back forcefully against Trump's threat of 100% tariffs on French wine, declaring that Europe sets its own laws. The clash over France's 3% digital services tax on US tech firms threatens to overshadow the G7 summit in Évian.

item.title: 🇷🇺🛢️ Moscow and St. Petersburg Impose Fuel Purchase Limits After Ukrainian Refinery Strikes
item.description: Russian gas stations in Moscow and St. Petersburg have introduced purchase limits — restricting customers to 20 liters of gasoline — following successful Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil refineries that disrupted domestic fuel supply chains.

item.title: ⚔️ France 24: AI, Drones and Ukrainian Tech on Display at Eurosatory Defense Fair
item.description: Over 2,500 exhibitors are showcasing cutting-edge military technology including AI systems, drones, and Ukrainian battlefield innovations at the Eurosatory defense exhibition near Paris, one of the world's largest arms trade events.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Al-Monitor: Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan to Visit Moscow for Ukraine, Black Sea Talks
item.description: Turkey's top diplomat Hakan Fidan will travel to Moscow to reiterate Ankara's offer to host Russia-Ukraine peace negotiations, as the Black Sea security situation remains a critical concern for NATO.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France 24: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff on French Wine and Champagne Over Digital Tax
item.description: President Trump warned the US will impose 100% tariffs on French wine and champagne unless Paris removes its digital services tax on American tech giants, escalating trade tensions on the eve of the G7 summit in France.

item.title: 🇬🇧 SCMP: UK to Ban Under-16s From Social Media Apps Including TikTok and YouTube
item.description: Britain announced some of the world's strictest online safety measures for children, banning social media access for under-16s with additional restrictions for all teenagers — going further than Australia's similar legislation.

item.title: 🏛️ Politico: Three Things to Watch as Trump Strikes Iran Deal
item.description: Markets are surging and oil prices plunging after Trump announced a tentative peace deal with Iran, but Politico outlines key challenges ahead for the administration as it heads into G7 talks in France.

item.title: 🇫🇷 G7 leaders convene in France as Trump tariff threats and Iran deal dominate agenda
item.description: Leaders of the Group of Seven wealthy nations arrive at a French lakeside resort for a summit overshadowed by Trump tariff threats and the newly announced US-Iran peace agreement.

item.title: ⛪ Russian strike sets fire to 1,000-year-old Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery
item.description: A major Russian overnight attack sparked an intense blaze at the UNESCO-listed Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, a symbol of Ukrainian spiritual history founded in 1051. France compared the attack to bombing Notre Dame.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US-Iran framework agreement defers most contentious issues, full text unpublished
item.description: The full text of the US-Iran deal has not been published. Initial details suggest the framework defers the hardest questions including nuclear oversight and sanctions sequencing, with formal signing set for Friday in Switzerland.

item.title: ⛏️ Trump critical minerals pricing plan faces skeptical G7 allies and divided industry
item.description: The Trump administration push to form a Western critical minerals trading bloc and regulate prices faces skepticism from G7 partners and divisions within the US mining industry over costs and a Pentagon AI pricing system.

item.title: 📈 Tentative Iran war deal sends stocks soaring and oil prices tumbling
item.description: Asian shares surged and oil prices fell sharply after the US and Iran announced a framework deal to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with Brent crude dropping below $85 a barrel.

item.title: 🇪🇸🕊️ Spain's Sanchez hails US-Iran deal as end to "senselessness" and new era for Middle East
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez called the US-Iran agreement the end of "senselessness" and the start of a new era in the Middle East, urging the world to remember the costs of war and learn from the conflict.

item.title: 🇵🇸📉 West Bank economy teetering toward collapse under Israeli restrictions, report finds
item.description: A new report says Israel's web of restrictions is systematically dismantling the economy of the occupied West Bank, pushing it toward collapse and severely limiting economic opportunities for Palestinians.
